tasks.subject: [chat-input-always-editable] [P1] Decouple disabled states and make textarea always editable during streaming
tasks.description:
## Objective

Decouple the single `isDisabled` boolean in `ChatInput.tsx` into two separate concerns: input acceptance and submit readiness. This allows the textarea to remain editable during streaming while still blocking submission.

## Files to Modify

- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatInput.tsx`

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Replace the single `isDisabled` boolean (current line 187)

**Current code:**
```typescript
const isDisabled = isLoading || sessionBusy;
```

**Replace with:**
```typescript
const isInputDisabled = sessionBusy; // Only server lock disables textarea
const isSubmitDisabled = isLoading || sessionBusy; // Blocks submission
```

### 2. Update textarea disabled prop (current line 250)

Change from `disabled={isDisabled}` to `disabled={isInputDisabled}`. This means the textarea stays editable during streaming (`isLoading=true`) but locks when another client holds the session (`sessionBusy=true`).

### 3. Update paperclip button disabled prop (current line 220)

Change from `disabled={isDisabled}` to remove the `disabled` prop entirely. Users should always be able to stage files for the next message, even during streaming.

### 4. Update clear button visibility logic (current line 184)

**Current:**
```typescript
const showClear = hasText && !isLoading && !sessionBusy;
```

**New:**
```typescript
const showClear = hasText && !sessionBusy;
```

The clear button should work whenever text exists, regardless of streaming state. Users need to be able to clear their draft while watching the agent work.

### 5. Guard Enter key submission (current line 111-116)

**Current `handleKeyDown`:**
```typescript
if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey && !isMobile) {
  e.preventDefault();
  if (!isLoading && value.trim()) {
    onSubmit();
  }
}
```

**New ‚Äî add `sessionBusy` guard:**
```typescript
if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey && !isMobile) {
  e.preventDefault();
  if (!isLoading && !sessionBusy && value.trim()) {
    onSubmit();
  }
}
```

This ensures Enter does nothing during streaming (Phase 1 behavior). Phase 2 will change Enter to queue during streaming.

### 6. Update send button disabled logic (current line 273)

The send button's `disabled` prop currently uses `!showButton || sessionBusy`. Since `showButton` already includes `isLoading`, the button correctly stays visible but the onClick logic routes to `onStop` when loading. No change needed for the send button disabled prop, but verify the pointer-events-none class uses `isSubmitDisabled` for the non-loading case.

## Testing Requirements

Update `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/__tests__/ChatInput.test.tsx`:

### Tests to modify

1. **"disables textarea when loading"** ‚Äî This test currently asserts `disabled=true` when `isLoading=true`. This test must be CHANGED to expect `disabled=false` when only `isLoading=true` (textarea is now always editable during streaming). Rename to "does NOT disable textarea when loading".

2. **"hides clear button when loading"** (line 270-274) ‚Äî This test asserts clear is hidden during loading. CHANGE to assert clear is VISIBLE during loading (only hidden when sessionBusy).

### Tests to add

3. Add test: "textarea is NOT disabled during streaming" ‚Äî render with `isLoading={true}`, assert `disabled` is `false`.

4. Add test: "textarea IS disabled when sessionBusy is true" ‚Äî render with `sessionBusy={true}`, assert `disabled` is `true`.

5. Add test: "paperclip button is NOT disabled during streaming" ‚Äî render with `isLoading={true}` and `onAttach` callback, assert paperclip button does not have `disabled` attribute.

6. Add test: "clear button works during streaming" ‚Äî render with `value='hello'` and `isLoading={true}`, assert clear button is interactive (no `pointer-events-none` class).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Textarea remains editable when `isLoading=true` (streaming)
- [ ] Textarea is disabled when `sessionBusy=true`
- [ ] Enter key does not submit during streaming
- [ ] Clear button works during streaming
- [ ] Paperclip button works during streaming
- [ ] Send button correctly shows stop icon during streaming
- [ ] All existing tests pass (with modifications noted above)
- [ ] New tests written and passing
tasks.activeForm: Decoupling textarea disabled states from submit readiness

tasks.subject: [chat-input-always-editable] [P3] Implement arrow key queue navigation with cursor position gating
tasks.description:
## Objective

Implement shell-history-style arrow key navigation in `ChatInput.handleKeyDown` that allows users to navigate through queued messages using Up/Down arrows, gated by cursor position and palette state.

## Files to Modify

- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatInput.tsx`

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Add queue navigation to handleKeyDown

Insert the following block **BEFORE** the palette-open interception block and **AFTER** the Escape handling:

```typescript
// Queue navigation (takes priority over palette when queue has items and palette is closed)
if (!isPaletteOpen && queueHasItems) {
  if (e.key === 'ArrowUp') {
    const textarea = textareaRef.current;
    const isAtStart = !textarea || textarea.selectionStart === 0;
    const isEmpty = !value.trim();
    if (isEmpty || isAtStart) {
      e.preventDefault();
      onQueueNavigateUp?.();
      return;
    }
  }
  if (e.key === 'ArrowDown') {
    const textarea = textareaRef.current;
    const isAtEnd = !textarea || textarea.selectionStart === textarea.value.length;
    if (editingQueueItem && isAtEnd) {
      e.preventDefault();
      onQueueNavigateDown?.();
      return;
    }
  }
}
```

### 2. Add Escape-to-cancel-edit

Insert before the existing Escape logic at the top of `handleKeyDown`:

```typescript
if (e.key === 'Escape' && editingQueueItem) {
  onCancelEdit?.();
  return;
}
```

This ensures that Escape while editing a queue item cancels the edit and restores the user's draft, rather than triggering the normal Escape behavior (dismiss palette / clear text).

### 3. Cursor position gating logic

- **ArrowUp** triggers navigation when:
  - The textarea is empty (no text), OR
  - The cursor is at position 0 (start of text)
  - This mirrors shell behavior: Up arrow at the start of input goes to history

- **ArrowDown** triggers navigation when:
  - The user is currently editing a queue item (`editingQueueItem` is true), AND
  - The cursor is at the end of the text
  - Down arrow only navigates forward when already in the queue

- **ArrowUp when palette is open** does NOT trigger queue navigation (palette takes priority).

### 4. Update handleKeyDown dependency array

Add the new props to the dependency array:
```typescript
[
  isStreaming,
  isMobile,
  value,
  onSubmit,
  onEscape,
  onClear,
  isPaletteOpen,
  onArrowUp,
  onArrowDown,
  onCommandSelect,
  editingQueueItem,
  onQueue,
  onSaveEdit,
  onCancelEdit,
  queueHasItems,
  onQueueNavigateUp,
  onQueueNavigateDown,
]
```

## Testing Requirements

Update `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/__tests__/ChatInput.test.tsx`:

### New test cases

1. `Up arrow navigates to queue when queue has items and textarea is empty` ‚Äî render with `queueHasItems={true}`, `value=''`, `isPaletteOpen={false}`, `onQueueNavigateUp=vi.fn()`, press ArrowUp, verify `onQueueNavigateUp` called.

2. `Up arrow navigates to queue when cursor is at position 0` ‚Äî render with `queueHasItems={true}`, `value='hello'`, `isPaletteOpen={false}`, `onQueueNavigateUp=vi.fn()`. Set cursor to position 0 via `selectionStart=0`. Press ArrowUp. Verify `onQueueNavigateUp` called.

3. `Up arrow does NOT navigate when cursor is in middle of text` ‚Äî render with `queueHasItems={true}`, `value='hello world'`, press ArrowUp. The textarea's default selectionStart is at the end, so this should NOT trigger navigation unless at position 0.

4. `Up arrow does NOT navigate when palette is open` ‚Äî render with `queueHasItems={true}`, `isPaletteOpen={true}`, `onQueueNavigateUp=vi.fn()`, `onArrowUp=vi.fn()`, press ArrowUp. Verify `onArrowUp` (palette) called, `onQueueNavigateUp` NOT called.

5. `Up arrow does NOT navigate when queue is empty` ‚Äî render with `queueHasItems={false}`, `value=''`, `onQueueNavigateUp=vi.fn()`, press ArrowUp. Verify `onQueueNavigateUp` NOT called.

6. `Down arrow navigates forward when editing queue item and at end of text` ‚Äî render with `queueHasItems={true}`, `editingQueueItem={true}`, `value='text'`, `onQueueNavigateDown=vi.fn()`, press ArrowDown. Verify `onQueueNavigateDown` called.

7. `Down arrow does NOT navigate when not editing queue item` ‚Äî render with `queueHasItems={true}`, `editingQueueItem={false}`, `onQueueNavigateDown=vi.fn()`, press ArrowDown. Verify `onQueueNavigateDown` NOT called.

8. `Escape cancels edit when editingQueueItem is true` ‚Äî render with `editingQueueItem={true}`, `onCancelEdit=vi.fn()`, `onEscape=vi.fn()`, press Escape. Verify `onCancelEdit` called and `onEscape` NOT called.

9. `Escape does NOT cancel edit when not editing` ‚Äî render with `editingQueueItem={false}`, `onCancelEdit=vi.fn()`, `onEscape=vi.fn()`, press Escape. Verify `onEscape` called and `onCancelEdit` NOT called.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ArrowUp navigates to queue when empty or cursor at start
- [ ] ArrowUp does NOT navigate when palette is open
- [ ] ArrowUp does NOT navigate when queue is empty
- [ ] ArrowDown navigates forward only when editing a queue item and cursor at end
- [ ] Escape cancels edit when editing a queue item
- [ ] Normal Escape behavior preserved when not editing
- [ ] All 9 new tests written and passing
- [ ] Existing arrow key tests still pass
